zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William de la Haye 2nd of Locherworth於1235年出生在蘇格蘭時，他的父親John de la Haye45歲，母親Margaret Lyne40歲。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1308年在他的家鄉享年73歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William de la Haye 2nd of Locherworth于1235年出生在苏格兰时，他的父亲John de la Haye45岁，母亲Margaret Lyne40岁。他至少有1个儿子。他去世于1308年在他的家乡享年73岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Haye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Helmut, Willi.

North German, East Frisian, and Dutch: from a personal name, Frisian Hajo, ancient Germanic Haio; or from a short form of the female name Hadewig, composed of the elements hadu ‘battle, strife’ + wīg ‘war’.
German: variant of   Hey   4, 5.

variants.name: Deshayes
meanings: French: topographic name for someone who lived by an enclosure, from the plural of Old French haye ‘hedge’ (see Haye and Hayes ), with fused preposition and definite article des ‘from the’; or a habitational name denoting someone from Les Hayes, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare Deshaies .

variants.name: Hayenga
meanings: East Frisian: patronymic from the personal name Hajo (see Haye 1). It is formed with the suffix -enga, a variant of -inga (from the patronymic suffix -ing and genitive plural ending -a).

variants.name: Hey
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hay  .
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Heij, a topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, Dutch hei, heide.
German: metonymic occupational name for a grower or mower of grass, from Middle High German höu ‘grass, hay’.

variants.name: Hayen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphonse.  German Heinz, Wilhelm.
Flemish, East Frisian, and North German: patronymic from   Haye   1.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William de la Haye 2nd of Locherworth was born in 1235, in Scotland, his father, John de la Haye, was 45 and his mother, Margaret Lyne, was 40. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1308, in his hometown, at the age of 73.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William de la Haye 2nd of Locherworth est né en 1235, à Écosse, son père, John de la Haye, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Margaret Lyne, avait 40 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1308, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 73 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William de la Haye 2nd of Locherworth 1235 in Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater John de la Haye 45 und seine Mutter Margaret Lyne 40. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1308 im Alter von 73 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William de la Haye 2nd of Locherworth è nato il nel 1235 nel Scotland, il suo padre, John de la Haye, aveva 45 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Lyne, aveva 40 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1308, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 73 anni.

ja.Loquhariot, Peeblesshire, Scotland: イギリススコットランドペエブレシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Sir William de la Haye 2nd of Locherworthが1235年にスコットランドで生まれた時，父John de la Hayeは45歳，母Margaret Lyneは40歳であった。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1308年に故郷で73歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William de la Haye 2nd of Locherworth(이)가 1235년 스컷랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de la Haye(은)는 45세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Lyne(은)는 40세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1308년 그의 고향에서 73세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William de la Haye 2nd of Locherworth nasceu em 1235, em Scotland, seu pai, John de la Haye, tinha 45 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Lyne, tinha 40 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1308, em sua cidade natal, com 73 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William de la Haye 2nd of Locherworth родился в 1235 г. в местности Шотландия, его отцу по имени John de la Haye было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Lyne, 40. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1308 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 73 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William de la Haye 2nd of Locherworth nació en 1235, en Scotland, su papá, John de la Haye, tenía 45 años y su mamá, Margaret Lyne, tenía 40 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1308, en su pueblo, a la edad de 73 años.
